lawTitle: Section 85 & 86 of Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS) / Sec 498A IPC
actualText: Punishes the husband or relative of the husband of a woman who subjects her to physical or mental cruelty with imprisonment up to three years and a fine.
simpleExplanation: Criminalizes physical or mental cruelty against a wife. It is frequently misused to file vague, omnibus allegations designed to drag in a husband's aging parents, married sisters, and distant relatives who live separately.
context: A mandatory procedural notice must be served first. Automatic, immediate arrests upon filing an FIR are illegal.

favorableJudgments.citation: Arnesh Kumar v. State of Bihar (2014) 8 SCC 273
favorableJudgments.ruling: The Supreme Court held that police cannot automatically arrest the husband or his relatives in 498A cases. Issuing a notice of appearance is mandatory. Unnecessary arrests violate civil liberties.

favorableJudgments.citation: Preeti Gupta & Anr. v. State of Jharkhand (2010) 7 SCC 667
favorableJudgments.ruling: The Supreme Court noted that implication of distant relatives in 498A cases is rampant. The court quashed the case against the married sister-in-law and brother-in-law living in a different city.

immediateActionPlan: Secure a copy of the actual FIR from the police portal or station immediately.
Track down separate residential proofs (rental agreements, electricity bills, job location certificates) for family members falsely accused to prove they do not share a household.
Await the formal Section 35(3) BNSS / 41A CrPC notice from the police. Prepare a written, factual reply backed by timeline evidence.
If police pressure is heavy, immediately file for Anticipatory Bail in the Sessions Court or High Court to protect yourself and your family from custodial detention.

lawTitle: Section 144 of Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS) / Sec 125 CrPC
actualText: Empowers a Magistrate to order a person with sufficient means to pay a monthly allowance for the maintenance of his wife if she is unable to maintain herself.
simpleExplanation: Provides financial maintenance to separated wives to prevent destitution. However, the statute explicitly specifies exemptions: if a wife is living in adultery, refuses to live with her husband without a valid reason, or is highly qualified and voluntarily sitting idle to extort money, she is legally barred from claiming it.
context: The Supreme Court mandates that both parties must file a strict, comprehensive affidavit of assets and liabilities to expose hidden income tracks.

favorableJudgments.ruling: The Supreme Court laid down comprehensive guidelines to stop overlapping maintenance claims across different parallel courts and made a detailed assets and liabilities declaration mandatory for both spouses on oath.

favorableJudgments.citation: Damanpreet Kaur v. Indmeet Juneja (2012) Delhi HC
favorableJudgments.ruling: The High Court ruled that an educated, well-qualified wife holding professional degrees who was working in the past cannot choose to sit idle and force her husband to pay maintenance simply to penalize him.

immediateActionPlan: Collect and compile all tangible evidence of her educational qualifications (Degrees, CVs, LinkedIn profiles, past tax returns/Form 16).
File a formal discovery application under the law if you suspect she is actively working or concealing her income via cash/family bank accounts.
Draft a meticulous 'Affidavit of Assets and Liabilities' mapping your own true expenses, loans, and dependent family members to correctly lower your disposable income footprint.

lawTitle: Section 24 of the Hindu Marriage Act (HMA), 1955
actualText: Allows either spouse to apply for temporary maintenance pendente lite and the expenses of the legal proceedings if they have no independent income sufficient for their support.
simpleExplanation: Provides temporary monetary relief while a divorce or child custody case is pending in a Family Court. While textually gender-neutral, courts heavily scrutinize requests to ensure a capable, earning spouse does not abuse it to collect parallel stipends.
context: If a wife files multiple parallel maintenance requests under the DV Act, Sec 125 CrPC, and HMA, judges are legally bound to adjust or deny overlapping amounts.

favorableJudgments.citation: Sanjay Bhardwaj & Ors. v. State (2010) Delhi HC
favorableJudgments.ruling: The court ruled that a husband cannot be forced to beg or borrow money to pay maintenance to an equally qualified wife when he himself is unemployed and has no source of income.

immediateActionPlan: If she has filed parallel maintenance demands in separate courts, present copies of those petitions immediately to the judge.
Cite current precedent regarding overlapping maintenance adjustments so the court subtracts or completely dismisses dual payouts.

lawTitle: Section 35(3) of Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS) / Sec 41A CrPC
actualText: Mandates that in all cases where the arrest of a person is not reasonably required under law, the police officer must issue a formal notice directing the accused person to appear before them.
simpleExplanation: A vital procedural safeguard. For any offense carrying a maximum jail term of under 7 years (such as a typical domestic dispute under 498A IPC / 85 BNS), police cannot arrest you out of nowhere. They must give you a written warning to join the investigation. If you comply, you cannot be arrested.
context: Always join the investigation with legal representation and secure written acknowledgment from the police station.

favorableJudgments.citation: Satender Kumar Antil v. CBI (2022) 10 SCC 51
favorableJudgments.ruling: The Supreme Court emphasized that non-compliance with arrest protection procedures (like Section 41A CrPC) directly entitles the accused to bail without custodial lockup.

immediateActionPlan: Do not avoid the police station when this notice is issued. Avoiding it gives the police a legal excuse to state you are not cooperating and execute an arrest.
Attend with your advocate, present your factual timeline documents, and ensure you obtain a signed, stamped receipt showing your full attendance and compliance on that day.

lawTitle: Section 379 of BNSS / Section 340 of Criminal Procedure Code (CrPC)
actualText: Establishes the procedure to prosecute individuals who commit perjury, fabricate false evidence, or file deliberate lies/forgeries within judicial proceedings.
simpleExplanation: The core countermeasure against lying under oath. If a spouse submits completely manufactured income bills, hides active employment, or signs a completely false statement in court to extort higher alimony, the husband can trigger an investigation against her.
context: If perjury is proven, the court itself becomes the complainant, prosecuting the individual for misleading the justice system.

favorableJudgments.citation: Kishan Lal v. State of Rajasthan (2023)
favorableJudgments.ruling: Courts have repeatedly affirmed that an application under Section 340 CrPC must be entertained if a party knowingly introduces deliberate fabrications to distort court orders.

immediateActionPlan: The exact moment you catch your spouse lying on a signed, verified court affidavit, do not wait for the final judgment.
Instruct your defense advocate to file a dedicated interim application under Section 379 BNSS / 340 CrPC detailing the exact document falsification.
Demand a formal judicial inquiry into her statements to use as leverage against malicious extortion demands.
